info.title: FDC+ Serial Drive Server API
info.description:
REST API for the Altair 8800 FDC+ Serial Drive Server web interface. Manages disk image serving, CP/M filesystem browsing, cassette audio, terminal serial connections, script replay, and XMODEM transfers.

## Socket.IO Events

The server also exposes real-time events via Socket.IO (not covered by OpenAPI).

### Server → Client

| Event | Payload | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `status` | `ServerStatus` | Periodic server status broadcast (1s interval) |
| `terminal:data` | `number[]` | Incoming serial data as byte array |
| `terminal:status` | `TerminalStatus` | Terminal connection status change |
| `terminal:error` | `{message: string}` | Terminal error |
| `replay:progress` | `ReplayProgress` | Transfer progress update |
| `replay:status` | `{active, mode, progress}` | Transfer status on connect |

### Client → Server

| Event | Payload | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `request-status` | _(none)_ | Request current server status |
| `terminal:write` | `string` | Send keyboard input to terminal serial port |
| `terminal:control` | `{type, value}` | Set DTR/RTS control signals |
| `replay:start` | `ReplayStartParams` | Start raw replay or XMODEM send |
| `replay:cancel` | _(none)_ | Cancel active transfer |

lastIo.description: Epoch ms of most recent successful read/write; null if no I/O yet.

build.description: Debian revision derived at build time from git; null in dev builds.

commit.description: Short SHA of HEAD when the .deb was built; null in dev builds.

dirty.description: True when the working tree had uncommitted changes at build time.

builtAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC build timestamp; null in dev builds.

get.description: Returns terminal serial port connection state, device, config, and preferred settings.
Enumerates serial ports available for the terminal connection.

post.description: Open a serial port for the terminal connection.

config.description: Serial port configuration (baud, dataBits, stopBits, parity)

post.description: Close the terminal serial port connection.

put.description: Update serial port configuration (baud, data bits, stop bits, parity) for an open terminal connection.

get.description: Enumerates serial ports on the host, including persistent device paths.

put.description: Change the serial device and baud rate for the primary FDC connection. Will close and reopen the port if needed.

get.description: Returns all files in the scripts directory with name and size.

post.description: Create a new script file. Fails if the file already exists.

get.description: Returns script metadata and content. Text files (.txt) include content; binary files return metadata only.

put.description: Overwrite the content of an existing script file.

post.description: Upload any file to the scripts directory. Max 1MB.
Start a raw text replay or XMODEM binary transfer of a script file over the terminal serial port.

interByteDelayMs.description: Delay between bytes in ms (raw mode)

interLineDelayMs.description: Delay between lines in ms (raw mode)

useCrc.description: Use CRC-16 instead of checksum (xmodem mode)

post.description: Cancel a running raw replay or XMODEM transfer.

get.description: Returns whether a transfer is active, its mode, and the last progress update.
Returns filenames of all disk images (.dsk, .img, .ima) in the disks directory.
Returns all disk images with file size, description, and notes.

post.description: Upload a disk image file (.dsk, .img, .ima). Max 10MB.
Create a copy of an existing disk image with a "-copy" suffix.
Create a new empty disk image in the specified format.

filename.description: Base filename (alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens, periods, spaces)

format.description: Disk format: 8inch = 8-inch floppy (77 tracks, 330K), minidisk = 5.25" mini-disk (17 tracks, 75K), 8mb = 8 MB hard disk (1863 tracks, ~7.8 MB)

delete.description: Delete a disk image file. Fails if the image is currently mounted on any drive.

409.description: File is mounted on a drive

post.description: Erases every byte on the image and lays down a fresh MITS 8" sector layout. Fails if the disk is mounted.

format.description: Target format. Omit to derive from the current image size.

put.description: Set or update the description and notes for a disk image.

Rename a disk image file. Fails if the image is mounted on any drive, if the target filename already exists, or if the new name contains path separators / traversal sequences. Carries the description and notes over to the new filename.

newFilename.description: Desired new filename (basename only, no slashes)

409.description: Mounted, or target name already exists

get.description: Returns server health status and current timestamp.
Returns serial connection state, disk serving state, drive statuses, and timestamp.

version.description: Upstream semver of the running build.

latestVersion.description: Newest release on GitHub, or null if the poll has not completed.

updateAvailable.description: True when latestVersion is strictly newer than version.

get.description:
Whitelisted past the auth middleware so the SPA can discover
what kind of authentication (if any) the daemon requires
before making its first authenticated call. Deliberately
reveals no state beyond the booleans — no key hint, no user
list, no session.

Two distinct booleans because the credentials are
independent:
- `loginRequired: true` when an `adminPassword` is set. The
  UI must prompt the operator to log in via
  `POST /api/auth/login`.
- `apiKeyRequired: true` when an `apiKey` is set. Machine
  clients (MCP over HTTP, curl) must pass
  `Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>`.

`authRequired` is retained for one release as a legacy alias
equal to `loginRequired || apiKeyRequired` so the previous
AuthGate contract keeps working across an in-place upgrade.
Returns mount status, filename, read-only flag, head-loaded state, and current track for each drive.

post.description: Mount a disk image file onto the specified drive slot.

400.description: Missing filename or invalid drive ID

post.description: Unmount the disk image from the specified drive slot.

put.description: Toggle write protection on a mounted drive. May remount the file with the correct mode.

post.description: Start the FDC server to serve disk images over serial. Opens serial port and begins listening for Altair commands.
Stop the FDC server and close the primary serial port.

get.description: Returns CP/M disk parameters, free space, file count, and mount status.

mounted.description: Drive number if mounted, false otherwise

get.description: List all files on the CP/M disk image.

post.summary: Upload file to CP/M disk image
post.description: Write a file into the CP/M filesystem on the disk image. Fails if the disk is currently mounted. Max 256KB.

400.description: No file uploaded or invalid filename

get.description: Extract and download a single file from the CP/M disk image. The cpmFile param format is "USER:NAME.EXT" (e.g. "0:ASM.COM").

parameters.name: cpmFile
parameters.description: CP/M file identifier in format USER:NAME.EXT

404.description: Disk image or CP/M file not found

delete.description: Remove a file from the CP/M filesystem on the disk image. Fails if the disk is currently mounted.

parameters.name: cpmFile
parameters.description: CP/M file identifier in format USER:NAME.EXT

404.description: Disk image or CP/M file not found

get.description: Returns current runtime configuration. Never includes `apiKey` — call `GET /api/config/status` to check whether it's set.

post.description: Legacy endpoint. Currently accepts `verbose` only. All other knobs must go through the per-section PUT endpoints and require a restart.

get.summary: JSON Schema for the config document
get.description: Emitted from the same Zod source of truth used to validate writes. Frontend renders field constraints from this — no duplicate schemas in the SPA.

Metadata the UI needs to decide whether the "Restart now" button
is available, whether saves will work, and how to detect a
successful restart (poll `startupEpoch`).

Returns the configured `apiKey` verbatim so an authenticated
operator can copy it back out at any time (password managers,
MCP client config, curl scripts). Unlike `adminPassword` — which
is bcrypt-hashed and unrecoverable — the API key is stored as
plaintext, so it can be read back. Protected by the same
session-or-Bearer auth as every other `/api/*` route, so only an
authenticated admin can reach it. Returns `{ apiKey: null }` when
no key is set.

200.description: The current API key (or null if unset)

post.summary: Gracefully exit so systemd relaunches the daemon
post.description:
Requires `?confirm=1` — the click path is destructive enough
that a stray XHR shouldn't take the daemon down. Returns 501
when the process isn't systemd-managed (dev / docker); the
UI should render a copy-paste command in that case.

202.description: Restart scheduled — poll GET /api/config/status until startupEpoch changes

post.description:
Best-effort re-read of `verbose`, `debug`, and `logFile` from
the current on-disk config. Serial / web / GPIO changes still
need a full restart. Returns 200 with the set of fields that
actually took effect.

post.summary: Undo the last save by restoring `<config-file>.bak.1`
post.description:
Atomically swaps `.bak.1` in as the live config file and
shifts `.bak.2` → `.bak.1`, `.bak.3` → `.bak.2` so a second
rollback walks further back. Requires `?confirm=1`. Refused
in kiosk mode (`--config-readonly`).

200.description: Rolled back — includes the restored config and its new mtimeMs

409.description: No backup to roll back to

put.summary: Toggle MCP-over-HTTP for remote AI clients
put.description:
Persists `enableMcpHttp` and flips the runtime guard immediately —
no daemon restart. Refuses to enable when no API key is set (400).
Disabling drops any live MCP sessions.

put.summary: Update the Web & API section (includes apiKey and adminPassword)
put.description:
Same shape as the other section PUTs, but two fields need
special handling:

- `apiKey` is a machine token; when non-null the value is
  written verbatim. Change is applied live (no restart) — the
  auth middleware reads apiKey via a runtime callback.
- `adminPassword` is bcrypt-hashed here before the write. Only
  the hash reaches disk; the plaintext never persists. Sending
  `null` clears the hash; omitting the key leaves it unchanged.
  Change is applied live (no restart).

Fields that still require a daemon restart (webPort, webHost,
enabling/disabling the web listener) are surfaced via
`restartRequired: true` in the response. When the patch only
touched auth fields, `restartRequired: false`.

get.description: Returns all cassette WAV files with size, description, and notes.

post.description: Upload a cassette WAV file. Max 100MB.

put.description: Set or update the description and notes for a cassette file.

get.description: Stream the cassette WAV file for client-side playback.

post.description: Play the cassette WAV file through the server's audio output. Stops any currently playing audio first.
Stop any currently playing cassette audio on the server.

post.summary: Verify the admin password and issue a session cookie
post.description:
Body `{ password }`. Compared (bcrypt) against the
`adminPassword` hash in the runtime config. Success sets
`fdcSession=<id>; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax; Path=/;
Max-Age=2592000` and returns 200. Bad password returns 401.
When no admin password is configured, returns 400 with
`code: LOGIN_NOT_CONFIGURED`.

Reads the session ID from the `fdcSession` cookie, drops it
from the in-memory store, and returns a cleared cookie.
Idempotent: called without a cookie or with a stale one it
still returns 200 so a "logout" click from a browser whose
session already expired doesn't error.

Requires the current session (auth middleware handles it).
Body `{ oldPassword, newPassword }` — the OLD password must
still verify against the stored hash even though the caller
is already authenticated. This is the mitigation against a
stolen cookie being used to permanently lock the operator
out. On success, the new hash lands in the override config
(via writePartialConfig, so backup rotation applies) and
every OTHER session is destroyed — the calling browser
stays logged in.
